data_quality_notes: Used RLI data from https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/red-list-index.csv?tab=chart. RLI data sourced from OWID/World Bank. RLI ranges 0 (all extinct) to 1 (no species extinct). Higher RLI = better outcomes, consistent with positive direction hypothesis. Note: a 5-10 year lag effect was hypothesized but not explicitly tested here (contemporaneous matching used). Data Deficient species are excluded from RLI, biasing toward well-known vertebrates.
summary: The Red List Index (RLI) shows a partially_confirmed correlation with the Species Protection Index (SPI). Pearson r=0.151 (p=0.0000), Spearman rho=0.112 (p=0.0000), n=5632 observations across 176 countries. Partial correlation controlling for log(GDP/capita): r=0.147 (p=0.0000). Best functional form: log-linear. The RLI measures average extinction risk and is expected to correlate positively with SPI (Species Protection Index), as well-protected areas should reduce extinction pressure. A lag effect of 5-10 years may attenuate contemporaneous correlations.
